task_completion.description: Verify the agent removed orphaned post metadata from the wp_postmeta table.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a MySQL query (e.g., 'DELETE FROM wp_postmeta WHERE post_id NOT IN (SELECT ID FROM wp_posts);') or an equivalent wp-cli command in the terminal, followed by a success message indicating rows were affected/deleted.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent cleared all transient options from the wp_options table.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the command 'wp transient delete --all' or a MySQL DELETE query targeting option_names containing '_transient' in the terminal, along with a success confirmation.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent deleted all post revisions from the wp_posts table.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a wp-cli command deleting revisions (e.g., 'wp post delete $(wp post list --post_type=revision --format=ids)') or a MySQL query like 'DELETE FROM wp_posts WHERE post_type = "revision";' with a success message.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent changed the autoload value of the '_legacy_theme_cache_data' option to 'no' or 'off'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a MySQL UPDATE query (e.g., 'UPDATE wp_options SET autoload = "no" WHERE option_name = "_legacy_theme_cache_data";') or a wp-cli command updating this specific option's autoload value to 'no', 'off', or '0'.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent dropped the unused 'wp_abandoned_plugin_logs' table.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the MySQL command 'DROP TABLE wp_abandoned_plugin_logs;' or an equivalent wp-cli db query, followed by a 'Query OK' or success response.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent checked that the front-end website remains fully functional.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a browser window displaying the WordPress front-end homepage without any database or PHP errors, or a successful HTTP request (e.g., curl) to the site URL returning HTML content.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent actually interacted with the database via MySQL shell, wp-cli, or a GUI tool.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for actual terminal prompts, MySQL shell interfaces, or WordPress admin screens showing command execution and system responses, rather than just typing SQL commands into a text editor.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent did not delete the '_legacy_theme_cache_data' option entirely.
integrity.visual_evidence: Verify that the command used for '_legacy_theme_cache_data' was an UPDATE command (or wp option update) and NOT a DELETE command.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent actively verified the front-end functionality.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for a screenshot where the agent navigates to the public-facing WordPress site in a browser or uses a network tool like curl to fetch the page, proving they didn't just assume it worked.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target table to drop is exactly 'wp_abandoned_plugin_logs'. The target option to modify is exactly '_legacy_theme_cache_data'. The expected autoload value for this option must be changed to 'no', 'off', or '0'. The option must NOT be deleted.
